content.problemStatement:
"I hide in plain sight, just as you".

Gus Fring has earned a huge profit from Los Pollos Hermanos and he wishes to celebrate. He loves watching horse-riding, hence he bought NNN horses.
One day he organizes a horse-riding competition to celebrate the deal with Don Eladio.

There are NNN horses taking part in the competition with speeds of AAA111,,, AAA222,,, ...,...,..., AAANNN respectively. It is given that all speeds are **distinct**.

Multiple races are being conducted and it is **not mandatory** for each horse to participate in every race.

For the iiiththth horse, find the number of subsets with size ≥\geq≥ 222 such that if a race were to be conducted between horses in this subset then the iiiththth horse would finish in the **top three**.

For subsets of size 2 the runner should finish either 1st or 2nd. There is no 3rd position when the size of the subset is 2.

Since the answer might be quite large, compute it, modulo 100000000710000000071000000007 (109+7)(10^9+7)(109+7).

###Input:


First line will contain TTT, number of testcases. Then the testcases follow.
First line of each testcase contains of a single integer NNN denoting the number of horses.
The second line of each testcase contains NNN space-separated integers AAA111,,, AAA222,,, ...,$$ANNN, denoting the speeds of the horses.
###Output:
For each testcase, output a single line containing NNN space separated integers , where the iiiththth integer denotes the number of subsets of size ≥\geq≥ 222 modulo (109+7)(10^9+7)(109+7), such that the iiiththth horse finishes in the top three.

###Constraints


1≤T≤50001 \leq T \leq 50001≤T≤5000
2≤N≤1052 \leq N \leq 10^52≤N≤105
1≤A[i]≤1091 \leq A[i] \leq 10^91≤A[i]≤109
All speeds are distinct
Sum of N over all test cases does not exceed 5∗1055*10^55∗105
